key_departures.impact: Released on first day of new league year. Falcons take on $22.5M dead money (spread across two years as a post-June 1 cut). Contract subject to up to $10M in offsets if Cousins signs elsewhere. Leaves QB room empty aside from recovering Michael Penix Jr.

Cut to free up cap space. Was scheduled for an $18.4M cap hit. Had one strong season in 2024 before an injury-marred 2025. Leaves a massive hole in the WR room with Drake London as the only reliable option.
Released to save $2.64M in cap space. Four-year Falcon and special teams ace. Departure further thins WR room and weakens special teams.
Did not re-sign. Defensive MVP of the team by many accounts — 380 tackles, 12.5 sacks, 29 TFLs across three years in Atlanta. Major hole at off-ball LB with Andersen coming off PUP.

Lost to Jets in free agency. Was a reliable run-stuffer who appeared in 48 of a possible 51 games over three seasons (157 combined tackles, 8.0 sacks, 21 TFLs). Leaves a clear hole in the interior defensive line.

Signed with Bills on 3-year, $21M deal ($10.12M guaranteed). Had a breakout 2025 — started 11 games filling in for injured Billy Bowman Jr., posted career-highs in INTs (3), PBUs (13), and sacks (2). Versatile slot/outside CB who was key to Jeff Ulbrich's scheme. A significant secondary loss.
Signed with Cardinals (2yr/$12.25M). Leaves Bijan Robinson as the clear RB1 but the depth behind him is now thin. Was seeking a starting role and ATL couldn't justify his price as a backup.
Not re-signed. Had 3.5 sacks on a 1-year/$10M deal in 2025. Departure further depletes an EDGE room already battered by James Pearce Jr.'s legal situation.
Not re-signed. Former second-round pick had 16.5 sacks over four years in Atlanta. Departure with James Pearce Jr.'s suspension uncertainty creates a critical pass rush need.
Not re-signed despite being a team captain and one of Atlanta's few special teams bright spots in 2025. Replaced by Jake Bailey signing.

signings.analysis: Dolphins released Tagovailoa and took on a record ~$100M dead cap hit, meaning any team signing him gets him at the $1.3M league minimum due to offset language. One-year deal with no real investment signals bridge/stopgap only. Tua is the projected Week 1 starter while Penix recovers from ACL surgery. He led the league in passing yards (2023) and completion percentage (2024) but struggled in 2025 with injury history. This is a minimum-cost flier that partially addresses QB need in 2026 — but because it's a one-year minimum deal with zero guarantees beyond his salary, QB remains a critical draft/long-term need. The Falcons still need to develop/confirm Penix as their franchise QB.
signings.notes: Contract terms confirmed by multiple insider reports (Schefter, Rapoport, Pelissero). Deal not yet official as of March 10 — new league year opens March 11.

signings.analysis: 2-year deal worth up to $17M with $10M guaranteed (per Adam Schefter). Former first-round pick (16th overall, 2022) who has underperformed — career best was 523 yards as a rookie. Had just 18 catches for 262 yards in 2025 with Philadelphia. At $7.5M AAV this is a mid-tier depth/upside bet, not a proven WR2 investment. Two years of real money signals Atlanta sees him as a legitimate contributor, not a camp body. Provides speed (4.43 40) that was absent from the 2025 WR room. WR need is REDUCED but not resolved — London+Dotson+Zaccheaus is not a full WR corps. Draft investment still needed at WR.
signings.notes: Contract confirmed via Adam Schefter/ESPN. Deal not yet official as of March 10.

signings.analysis: Reunion with former Falcons UDFA (2019–2022). Terms not yet publicly disclosed beyond it being a short-term deal. Per ESPN, projects as a third or fourth receiver. Provides slot depth, special teams return value, and familiarity under president of football Matt Ryan. At 5-foot-8 and coming off a 39-catch/313-yard year with Chicago, this is a depth piece — not a starter-level solution. Minimally addresses the WR depth hole left by Mooney and Hodge cuts.
signings.notes: Contract terms not yet reported as of March 10. Confirmed signing via Tom Pelissero/NFL Network.

signings.analysis: 1-year, $3.25M deal (per NFL Network — some reports indicate $3.25M, one outlet notes $3.5M; using NFL Network figure as primary source). Reuniting with former Falcons (2016–2019) TE in what Kevin Stefanski (who also coached him in Cleveland 2020–21) clearly views as a trusted complementary piece. Projects as TE2 behind franchise-tagged Kyle Pitts. Had 21 catches for 263 yards and 2 TDs in reduced role with Patriots in 2025. At 31 on a 1-year deal, this is pure depth — but Hooper+Pitts gives Stefanski the 12-personnel sets he loves. TE was not a top need given Pitts' tag, so this is a plus move.
signings.notes: Spotrac confirms 1yr/$3.25M. Guarantees TBD — likely minimal given age and 1-year structure.

signings.analysis: 1-year, $3.1M deal (per Adam Schefter). Former Cardinals 3rd-round pick (2022) who spent time with Kansas City and Cleveland, where he worked under Kevin Stefanski. Projects as an inside/outside rotational DE similar to Zach Harrison — was not credited with a single missed tackle in 2025 and posted 2.5 sacks and 13 pressures in 300+ snaps. At $3.1M on a prove-it deal, this is a depth signing, not a pass rush solution. EDGE remains a critical need with Ebiketie and Floyd gone and Pearce Jr. facing a potential suspension.

Contract terms not yet reported — described broadly as 'affordable' and likely sub-$3M given Harris's status. Former Texans 3rd-round pick (2022) who was a full-time starter early in his career before injuries and inconsistent play pushed him to a backup role; missed most of 2024 with injury and never regained a starting job in 2025. Young (25) with upside but not a Kaden Elliss replacement. With Elliss departed and Andersen coming off a full year on PUP, Harris provides low-cost depth but LB remains a major draft/FA priority. Minimally addresses the gaping hole at inside linebacker.
signings.notes: Signing reported by Cameron Wolfe/NFL Network. Terms undisclosed as of March 10.

signings.analysis: 2-year deal (per Tom Pelissero). Total value and guarantees not yet disclosed. Addresses a brutal kicker situation after Atlanta used three different kickers in 2025 (Younghoe Koo, Parker Romo, Zane Gonzalez). Folk led the NFL in field goal percentage for three straight seasons, going 28/29 (96.6%) in 2025. Two-year investment signals a real solution. New special teams coordinator Craig Aukerman previously worked with Folk in Tennessee (2023). Kicker is fully resolved for 2026–2027.
signings.notes: 2-year structure confirmed by Tom Pelissero/NFL Network. Total value TBD.

signings.analysis: 3-year, $9M deal with $5M fully guaranteed (per Adam Schefter/ESPN). All-Pro punter in 2020 with New England Patriots; averaged 46.9 yards per punt with Dolphins. Three-year commitment with meaningful guarantees signals this is Atlanta's long-term punter, not a bridge. Replaces Bradley Pinion and is an upgrade in a complete special teams overhaul. New special teams coordinator Craig Aukerman overlapped with Bailey in Miami. P is fully resolved.
signings.notes: Contract confirmed by Adam Schefter/ESPN ($9M/3yr, $5M guaranteed).

signings.analysis: Franchise tag applied ($15.1M, fully guaranteed for 2026). Pitts was named second-team All-Pro in 2025, his best professional season. Tag resolves TE need for 2026 but long-term extension remains unresolved — team has indicated they want to see another year before committing. At $15.1M on a one-year tag, TE is addressed for this cycle. Kevin Stefanski's deployment of Pitts alongside Hooper in 12-personnel sets is a clear philosophical priority.
signings.notes: Franchise tag value confirmed at $15.1M (fully guaranteed) per multiple sources.

signings.analysis: Contract restructured to avoid a 'tolled' contract situation after Andersen missed all of 2025 on PUP. Former 2nd-round pick out of Montana State. Restructure retains him but he is not a proven starter coming off a full season lost to injury. His return is hopeful but uncertain — described as 'no lock to play and/or play well this year.' LB need remains elevated despite his retention. Provides injury-insurance depth but is not the Elliss replacement.
signings.notes: Restructure confirmed by Falcons On SI. New terms not disclosed.

signings.analysis: UDFA (2023, Southern Miss) extended by Falcons. Has made 26 game appearances with just one start across 2024–2025. Primarily a practice squad depth piece and special teamer. Does not move the needle on CB need.
signings.notes: Extension confirmed by official Falcons.com tracker. Terms not disclosed.

signings.analysis: Exclusive rights free agent tender. Originally signed off Giants practice squad in November 2025. ERFA tender retains him at minimum salary with no open-market rights. Camp/depth body only. Does not address the EDGE need.
signings.notes: ERFA tender confirmed by official Falcons.com tracker.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tua Tagovailoa signing (1yr/$1.3M minimum) partially addresses the immediate 2026 starter problem with a capable stopgap while Penix recovers from ACL surgery. However, because it is a league-minimum bridge deal with no multi-year commitment, QB remains a priority need in the draft — Atlanta must confirm Michael Penix Jr. as the long-term answer and still lacks a true developmental QB2 locked in beyond 2026. Drop from Tier 1 critical to Tier 2 — still draft-worthy in rounds 2–4 if the value is there.

Dotson (2yr/$15M, $10M gtd) and Zaccheaus (terms TBD, depth deal) address WR depth meaningfully but not completely. The WR corps is now London + Dotson + Zaccheaus + camp bodies — still no proven WR2, and Dotson is an upside bet, not a proven solution. Mooney and Hodge departures created this need. WR drops from Tier 1 emergency to Tier 2 — still a strong draft target, likely round 2.

Floyd and Ebiketie both departed, leaving Jalon Walker + James Pearce Jr. (facing 5 felony charges, potential suspension) + Cameron Thomas (rotational, 1yr/$3.1M) + Elijah Garcia (ERFA). The Thomas signing is a depth move, not a starter. With Pearce's playing status deeply uncertain, EDGE is now arguably the single biggest unaddressed roster need. Escalate to Tier 1 critical — should be a high draft priority (rounds 1–3) or additional FA signing needed.

Kaden Elliss (departed) was the defensive MVP of the team. The replacement corps is: Troy Andersen (restructured, missed all of 2025 on PUP, uncertain), Christian Harris (depth/rotational, terms undisclosed). No true starter-caliber replacement has been added. LB escalates to Tier 1 — must be addressed in the draft (rounds 2–4) and potentially more FA signings.

David Onyemata departed to Jets (was a reliable run-stuffer). Remaining IDL: Ruke Orhorhoro, Brandon Dorlus, Zach Harrison, Cameron Thomas (versatile inside/outside). No direct replacement for Onyemata's run-stuffing ability signed. IDL remains a Tier 2 need — particularly for a run-stopping nose tackle type. Should target IDL in mid-rounds of the draft.

Dee Alford's departure to Buffalo (3yr/$21M) was a significant secondary loss. Alford was the team's most versatile CB in 2025 and a clear contributor in Jeff Ulbrich's scheme. Remaining CBs: A.J. Terrell (starter), Billy Bowman Jr. (coming off Achilles injury), Cobee Bryant (depth), Clark Phillips III (depth), Natrone Brooks (depth/ST). No starter-caliber CB added in FA to replace Alford. CB escalates to Tier 1 — needs a starter-level CB2 opposite Terrell, ideally with slot/nickel versatility.

Kyle Pitts franchise tag ($15.1M, fully guaranteed) locks in an All-Pro TE1 for 2026. Austin Hooper (1yr/$3.25M) adds solid TE2 depth with familiarity under Stefanski and Matt Ryan. TE is fully addressed for this season. No draft priority needed — luxury pick only.

Tyler Allgeier signed with Arizona Cardinals (2yr/$12.25M). Remaining backs: Bijan Robinson (starter, elite), Nate Washington (backup). Atlanta now has a clear RB2 depth void. Bijan is the anchor but depth needs to be added. RB moves from Tier 3 to Tier 2 — mid-round draft pick or lower-cost FA still needed.

Nick Folk (2-year deal) resolves a chronic kicker problem. Folk has been the NFL's most accurate kicker three straight seasons (28/29 in 2025). After using three kickers in 2025, K is now fully resolved for 2026–2027. No draft action needed.
Jake Bailey (3yr/$9M, $5M gtd) replaces Pinion with a long-term commitment. All-Pro punter in 2020, 3-year deal signals genuine investment. P is fully resolved — no draft action needed.

key_additions.headline: Former Pro Bowl QB joins as minimum-cost bridge starter while Michael Penix Jr. recovers from ACL surgery
All-Pro TE retained on franchise tag; anchors Stefanski's TE-heavy offensive system
Former first-round pick brings 4.43 speed to a WR room being rebuilt around Drake London
All-Pro punter signed to multi-year deal as Falcons overhaul worst special teams unit in NFL
NFL's most accurate kicker (96.6% FG% in 2025) ends Atlanta's revolving-door kicker nightmare
Two-time Pro Bowl TE reunites with Falcons to give Stefanski his 12-personnel option alongside Pitts
Rotational DE familiar to Stefanski from Cleveland provides pass-rush depth on thin EDGE roster
Young former Texans starter adds depth at inside linebacker after Kaden Elliss departure
Original Falcons UDFA returns to provide slot depth and special teams versatility for new regime

key_losses.headline: Two-year/$90M disaster contract mercifully ends; Falcons absorb massive dead cap to move on
Team's defensive MVP departs without replacement — 380 tackles, 12.5 sacks across three Atlanta seasons
Breakout 2025 CB (3 INTs, 13 PBUs) cashes in with Bills; leaves major hole opposite A.J. Terrell
Reliable run-stuffing DT departs after three seasons; interior defensive line needs replenishment
Cut to clear cap space after an injury-marred 2025; accelerates WR room overhaul
Reliable RB2 earns starter money elsewhere; leaves thin depth behind Bijan Robinson
Second-round pick departs without re-signing, worsening a pass rush already gutted by Pearce Jr.'s legal situation

additional_context.james_pearce_jr_situation: 2025 first-round EDGE rusher (10.5 sacks as a rookie) faces five felony charges stemming from a domestic violence-related incident in Florida. His 2026 playing status is deeply uncertain and a potential suspension is expected. This dramatically impacts the EDGE need picture — he cannot be counted as a reliable contributor in need assessments.
additional_context.michael_penix_jr_recovery: Starting QB suffered ACL tear in Week 11 of 2025. Recovery timeline uncertain; may not be ready for Week 1. Tagovailoa signing is explicitly a bridge solution.
additional_context.draft_capital: Falcons hold only 5 picks in the 2026 NFL Draft and no first-round selection (traded for James Pearce Jr. in 2025). GM Ian Cunningham has stated 2026 will be the 'last year we ever have five picks.' Limited draft capital amplifies the importance of each remaining pick.
additional_context.new_regime: Kevin Stefanski (HC), Ian Cunningham (GM), Matt Ryan (President of Football). First offseason. Emphasized building around Penix long-term while addressing immediate needs on budget.
